input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this .NET project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to:

1. First explore the project structure to understand it
2. Set up and run code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.

data.content: Good — the project already includes `coverlet.collector`. Let me run the tests with coverage collection.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-ed33f11fcd6647b6b7b1286f78a4a6e8 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./coverage-results --no-build 2>&1 | tail -30 || dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./coverage-results 2>&1 | tail -30

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage collection using coverlet (which is already referenced in the test project), and then analyze the results.

I'll run tests with coverage using `dotnet test` with coverlet's collector, producing a Cobertura XML report that I can parse.
